policy.fallback_rule: Use lower quantization only when Q8_0 is not published for the model, or when an operator explicitly approves it for hardware-constrained pools.
policy.reason: Q8 keeps quality closer to the source model. Lower quantization is useful for bootstrap capacity, but should not define the long-term community network.

note: Curated GGUF allowlist. A pool may only load a model whose (model_id, sha256) is approved here. This is the primary defense against poisoned-model attacks: a peer cannot be instructed to download arbitrary weights, and a peer that downloads a file whose sha256 does not match refuses to serve it.

models.note: Q8 verified. Smallest bootstrap model; fits low-RAM community nodes.
Q8 preferred small model. sha256 must be pinned before peers serve it.
Q8 preferred small/medium bootstrap model. sha256 must be pinned before peers serve it.
